mnemonics: IoT IP Is Everywhere: Imagine Internet Protocol (IP) as the universal address system that allows every 'Thing' on the Internet of Things to be found and communicated with, just like every house has a unique street address.
keyPoints: IP provides a standardized way for all devices on a network, including IoT sensors and actuators, to have a unique address.
It enables end-to-end communication between IoT devices and other networks, including the internet.
IPv6 is particularly important for IoT due to its massive address space, capable of assigning an IP address to virtually every object.
commonMistakes: Assuming all IoT devices use proprietary or non-IP protocols; while many do for low-power needs, IP is the fundamental layer for integration into larger networks.
Believing the limited address space of IPv4 is sufficient for the scale of IoT, overlooking the critical necessity of IPv6 for future expansion.
